module.name: shortcut
module.description: A keyboard shortcut as a sequence of styled key caps. The key combination is the block body, written with "+" separators (e.g. Ctrl+Shift+P). Renders inline so it flows within text.
module.aiSpec:
Use to show a keyboard shortcut as styled <kbd> key caps (for docs, tutorials, help slides). Block module but renders INLINE, so it flows within a sentence rather than as its own block. The body is the key combo written with "+" between keys (e.g. `Ctrl+Shift+P`); each key becomes one cap. The body ALWAYS uses "+" to separate keys — the `separator` param only changes the glyph shown between caps (set it to "then" for sequential chords like Ctrl then K).
Parameters: `size` — sm | md | lg (default md): size of the key caps. `separator` — text shown between caps (default "+"); e.g. "then" for a key sequence. `color` — CSS colour that tints the cap text and its bottom lip (default blank = theme colours).
Example: <!-- @shortcut size: lg, color: #3b82f6 --> Ctrl+Shift+P <!-- @end -->

param.name: separator
param.label: Separator
param.desc: Glyph shown between caps; e.g. 'then' for a sequence.

param.name: color
param.label: Key color
param.desc: Tints the cap text and bottom lip; blank = theme colours.

snippet.label: Shortcut
snippet.description: Key combination rendered as styled keys. Body = the combo, joined with "+".

snippet.label: Shortcut (sequence)
snippet.description: A sequential chord: caps separated by "then" instead of "+".
